_comment: Single source of truth for the governed SDK surface allowlist (AC-074 / REQ-053). Both the Python suite (src/python/tests/test_surface.py) and the TypeScript suite (src/ts/tests/surface.test.ts) load THIS file â€” there is no per-binding duplicate literal, so the two bindings cannot carry divergent copies (cross-binding parity, P2). Each suite asserts its INTROSPECTED live command surface is a SUBSET of `allowlist` (P1, never equality) and that `core` is present and live. As of Slice 30 the four `read.*` members are LIVE: each suite introspects the `read` namespace and asserts the four verbs are present-in-the-live-surface (a removal fails), in addition to the subset check. 0.8.6 Slice 10 (OPP-5): `embed` is a governed read-path primitive (Engine.embed, same name in Py+TS) â€” HITL-signed 2026-06-26; it was shipped Python-only in 0.8.4 and is now brought to Pyâ†”TS parity. 0.8.19 Slice 10 (OPP-12 record-lifecycle Phase-1): `transition` and `purge` are net-new governed lifecycle verbs (Engine.transition/Engine.purge, same names in Py+TS) â€” the AC-074 governed-surface delta, HITL-SIGNED 2026-07-10 at the 0.8.19 Slice-40 close (delta also carries the non-command types `LifecycleState`/`IllegalTransitionError`/`NotLifecycleAddressableError`/`IdSpace`/`IdSpaceKind`, `PreparedWrite::Node` +`state`/+`reason`, and the `SearchHit.id` `u64â†’IdSpace` retype; recovery_denylist UNCHANGED â€” five names; Phase-2 read-view/validity/projection types excluded). Proposed in the Slice-0 design Â§10. ***0.8.20 Slice 5d (R-20-E4, erasure) â€” AWAITING HITL SIGN-OFF, NOT SIGNED.*** `erase_source`/`eraseSource` (Engine.erase_source in Python, Engine.eraseSource in TypeScript) is a net-new governed lifecycle verb: the erasure counterpart to `purge`. `purge` addresses a GOVERNED node by `logical_id`; `erase_source` addresses ANONYMOUS content (rows with no `logical_id`) by its provenance, which `purge` cannot reach â€” so before this delta an SDK-only consumer with no CLI on `PATH` could not discharge a deletion obligation over anonymous content at all (R-20-E4). The delta ALSO carries: the non-command report types `EraseReport` (Py `fathomdb._fathomdb.EraseReport`, TS `EraseReport`) and, on the Rust facade, the net-new public `SourceId` provenance newtype from Slice 5c (carried here per Slice-5c follow-up FU-5c-2) plus `ExciseReport` moving from the operator-gated re-export block to the always-present one (it is `erase_source`'s return type). `excise_source` REMAINS CLI-only and is deliberately NOT allowlisted â€” it stays the recovery seam, and it alone may address the engine's reserved `_`-prefixed provenance namespace. recovery_denylist UNCHANGED â€” still exactly the five REQ-054 names; `erase_source` is not one of them, so AC-041 is unaffected and was re-run green. This entry is a PROPOSAL recorded so the branch is not red; the orchestrator is escalating it for the AC-079 sign-off.
